design_philosophy.core_loop: Real-time combat with party AI control, resource management via ring menu system, character progression through level-up stat allocation and dual-branching class system
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing three-character party composition with AI-controlled allies while navigating time-based world mechanics (day/night cycle, weekly elemental effects) and tactical spell/weapon choices
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Assembling a custom three-hero party from six unique characters, each with distinct storylines, and guiding them through interconnected narratives toward claiming the Mana Sword and preventing world destruction
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy emphasis on player agency in character customization: six character choice upfront determines narrative branches; dual class system (Light/Dark at levels 18 and 38) creates playstyle divergence without affecting story; calendar system ties world events and enemy spawns to temporal mechanics rather than story progression; multiplayer restricted to two players despite three-party mechanic

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player directs stat allocation at every level-up, selects class paths (Light/Dark branches) affecting skill availability and stat progression, manages equipment and spell loadouts, controls which party member is active in real-time combat while AI handles companions

progression.upgrade_system: Level-based stat progression with player-directed allocation (one stat point per level to distribute freely); dual-branching class system at level 18 (mandatory Light/Dark choice) and level 38 (optional second class change requiring rare item); equipment upgrades via shops and loot
progression.unlock_mechanism: Character story routes unlock based on initial three-of-six selection at game start; class changes unlock at fixed levels and Mana Stone locations; rare items gate advanced class choices; time-based events (day/night, weekly cycles) unlock different encounters and story triggers
replayability_hooks: Six playable characters with unique storylines incentivize multiple playthroughs with different party compositions
Dual Light/Dark class branches create distinct playstyle variations for each character
Three overlapping main plotlines offer narrative variety based on character selection
Time-based world mechanics (elemental days, day/night enemies) and calendar-gated events provide seasonal content discovery

world_building_techniques: Multiple protagonist storylines: three main plot routes determined by character selection, creating overlapping narrative structure
Environmental storytelling through time-based world changes (day/night cycles affecting NPC availability, enemy types, and story-gated events)
Calendar mechanics tied to narrative: weekly elemental themes, temporal progression gates story events
Character-specific dialogue and encounters: six character choices each have distinct narrative arcs and relationships
High fantasy setting with Benevodons (legendary destructive entities) and Mana Sword lore as overarching threat

faction_indicators: Light/Dark class alignment system creates character-level philosophical branching without story impact
Six playable characters with distinct backgrounds suggest faction or origin affiliations (not detailed in sources)
danger_signals: Benevodons positioned as world-ending threat requiring party to prevent their unleashing
Time-based encounter scaling (different enemies at different times) suggests environmental danger progression
environmental_storytelling: Day/night cycle affects NPC availability and story progression (nighttime-exclusive events like black markets)
Weekly elemental spirits tied to calendar system create living world perception
Character Kevin's werewolf transformation at night mechanically reinforces fantasy setting and time mechanics
Mana Stone locations scattered throughout world serve as mechanical and narrative anchors for class progression
